session_note: S62 - N-player multiplayer (2->N FFA, <=6; ship verified 1v1v1). Full-tier batch PDR, user-approved ('approved! work thoroughly'). 3-way Council (grok-4.20-reasoning + gemini-2.5-pro), R1 Trident + R2 convergence + PRIME-AUDIT delta.
council_battle_ledger: S62 Full 2-round Council. Grok(risk): ordered-roster ARRAY not Map (determinism); DON'T blanket-swap isNetworked - connection-lost unsafe (per-role host-loss not peerCount===0); global Player[6] array OVERRULED on feasibility (930 tests lean on Map; determinism via seat-order insertion); host-migration/reconnect/6p-bandwidth Critical/High -> deferred carry-forward; 5-priorities-delusional -> testable-slice. Gemini(quality): lobby 2/5 + color-only identity = UX gaps (swatches/who's-in/leaderboard-HUD/shape-icons) -> P5 polish + 4-6-phase carry. Resolutions: CONCEDED->GROK ordered-roster/connection-lost/scope-slice; SYNTHESIS Map+seat-order-insert / stamp+existing-reach-gates / HUD-as-leaderboard / honor-user-colors+defer-shape-icons; OVERRULED replace-Nostr.
prime_audit: S62 PRIME-AUDIT delta: NOT rubber-stamped Grok global Map->array (resolved on Claude feasibility - Map load-bearing across 930 tests; determinism via seat-order insert) nor replace-Nostr (out of scope). CAUGHT claim-addressed-not-fixed: connection-lost is a P1 MUST-implement per-role (client host-loss detection) not blanket isNetworked swap. Edge cases logged: intent-before-seat-ack (buffer), reconnect-orphaned-seat (release on leave; defer same-seat reclaim), 6p snapshot bandwidth (watch-item). Runtime-verifiability: arbiter = 3-peer Playwright e2e (P4) + live eyeball, not tsc-only. Hazardous risks = logged carry-forwards w/ justification, none silently dropped.
triumvirate_check: S58 batch CHECK (per-priority, tiered). P0/P1/P2 Micro = RALPH + Grok-analyst Lite (mechanical/test-harness/single-constant, all empirically green). P3/P4 Standard = Triumvirate-aligned (RALPH + replay-determinism guard + runtime e2e). Whole-batch end state: full unit 899/899, tsc -b clean, full e2e 10 pass/0 fail/1 skip (11 total) (Sym E fixme), build green. P4 (the S52-reversing capstone) runtime-verified via the new Sym J 2-peer e2e (claim propagates to host = no double-grab; release always exits = no glued-spark) with ALL regression gameplay e2e still green. Net new tests this session: +4 vision (own-creature) +1 sync (claim preserve) +1 e2e (Sym J). E2E gate on master restored (P0). USER action: live 2-peer cross-network smoke for carried-drag + fog feel (swiftshader-local cannot prove real-latency feel).

priorities.label: S62 P1 - Seat assignment + isNetworked() per-site audit-refactor: protocol v3->v4; new SEAT_ASSIGN msg (host->joiner seat idx); transport surfaces senderPeerId on receive; host allocates next free seat on peer-join + peerId<->seat map + STAMPS INTENT.playerId by sender seat (anti-collision) + buffers pre-seat intents; client adopts host-assigned seat; START_GAME_SIGNAL carries ORDERED roster array [seat->color]; insert players in seat order (deterministic Map iteration - NOT Map->array); introduce isNetworked(world) + audit each ~20 gameMode==='1v1' site - connection-lost reworked PER-ROLE (client host-loss); win-attribution(max-scan)/remote-reach(!==localPlayerId) safe to generalize.
priorities.check_method: S62 P1 (N-player core: seat assignment + isNetworked + roster seating) verified. protocol v3->v4 (START_GAME_SIGNAL carries ordered seat->color RosterEntry[]; parseNetMessage fail-closed validates roster). isNetworked(world)=gameMode!=='solo' replaces ~22 gameMode==='1v1' sites (behavior-identical today; connection-lost DELIBERATELY kept on peerCount===0 - host-correct, with logged client-host-loss P5 gap, no false-positive). Seat netcode: transport.peerIds()+selfId surfaced; host allocates seats by join order at Begin, freezes session.hostSeats peerId<->seat BEFORE broadcasting START (closes pre-ack race), stamps every INTENT.playerId by sender seat (intentStamp.ts pure helper, anti-spoof/anti-collision); client adopts host-assigned seat via Trystero selfId match (drops hardcoded id 1). EMPIRICAL: tsc -b exit 0; FULL unit 946/946 (+16); build 508.25 KB < 550 cap. Legacy 2-player path preserved (no-roster fallback keeps 1v1 tests green). checkpoint 7437f22.

priorities.label: S62 P2 - Roster seating + radial spawns + colors: applyStartGame seats ALL roster players (loop, seat order); radial spawn = pure fn of seat (base angle pi, R=SPAWNER_RADIUS+40; N=2 reproduces left/right); applyReturnToTitle keeps localPlayerId drops others; PLAYER_COLORS[2..5] -> yellow/green/orange/magenta (3-player crimson/cyan/yellow = no CVD collision).
priorities.check_method: S62 P2 (roster seating + radial spawns + colors) verified - shipped in the SAME commit as P1 (tightly coupled per Council). applyStartGame seats ALL roster players in SEAT ORDER (deterministic Map insertion = identical cross-client iteration) at radialSpawnPos (pure fn of seat: base angle pi, R=SPAWNER_RADIUS+40, Math.round so N=2 reproduces historical 670/1250 EXACTLY; all seats equidistant from spawner = FFA-fair). applyReturnToTitle resets to solo seat-0 identity (no dangling seat-N localPlayerId). PLAYER_COLORS[2..5] -> yellow(0xffe23b)/green(0x44ff5e)/orange(0xff8c1a)/magenta(0xd73bff); 3rd spark+buildings inherit yellow via centralized player.color. Unit-locked (nplayerSeating.test.ts, 15 tests): radialSpawnPos N=2-exact+equidistant+integer+distinct; applyStartGame seats-3+colors+seat-order+idempotent+legacy-2p+solo; applyReturnToTitle seat-2-client reset; stampSenderSeat override+anti-spoof+passthrough. checkpoint 7437f22 (shared).

priorities.label: S62 P3 - Minimal lobby-gate slice: lobby accepts N joiners, shows count, begin-gate >=2, wires ordered roster into START_GAME. NOT full lobby refactor (P5). Enough for testable 1v1v1.
priorities.check_method: S62 P3 (minimal N-seat lobby) verified. lobbyScreen: title '1v1 LOBBY'->'MULTIPLAYER LOBBY'; host peer-status shows LIVE connected count (was a one-shot 'Player 2 connected' latch) -> 'N players connected - press Begin Match (up to 6)', updates only on count CHANGE (no per-frame churn); begin-button reveals on >=1 peer (>=2 total). MINIMAL by Council design (NO constructor refactor - full N-seat UX swatches/ready/kick stays P5 polish). No new fields. EMPIRICAL: tsc -b clean; 29 lobby unit tests green (pure-helper, unaffected); the multi-peer begin flow runtime-verified by the P4 3-peer e2e (host sees 2 joiners, begins, all seated). checkpoint db9e81a.

priorities.label: S62 P4 - 3-peer e2e arbiter + live smoke: Playwright 3 contexts (host+2 joiners) assert seat assignment, 3 colors render, FFA scoring, first-to-score wins/others lose. Runtime boot-then-smoke. session goal = verified 1v1v1.
priorities.check_method: S62 P4 (3-peer e2e arbiter) verified - THE RUNTIME PROOF. New e2e/smoke.spec.ts '3-player FFA' test: host + 2 joiners over REAL Trystero/Nostr WebRTC; asserts distinct seats {0,1,2} (host=0), all 3 reach PLAYING w/ 3 players, cross-client COLOR AGREEMENT (crimson/cyan/yellow, seat 2 = yellow 0xffe23b), deterministic per-seat color on every peer, FFA scoring -> one winner seen by all. PASSED 26.6s. Also fixed the 2 protocol-mismatch e2e for v3->v4 (host v4; newer-peer v5). FULL E2E GATE GREEN: 16 pass / 1 skip (baseline + protocol x2 + Sym A/C/D/F/G/I/J + fog x5 + 3-peer FFA) = ZERO regression in 2-player gameplay from the isNetworked refactor + v4 bump. checkpoint db9e81a.

priorities.label: S62 P5 (Micro scope-amendment, user 'start polish') - N-player leaderboard HUD: ui.ts replaces the hardcoded 2-row RED/BLUE score readout with a pooled MAX_PLAYERS-row leaderboard (ranked by score, per-player color, local marked '> ... <YOU', charge dots aligned per row).
priorities.check_method: S62 P5 (N-player leaderboard HUD) verified. ui.ts: pooled MAX_PLAYERS Text rows replace hardcoded p1ScoreText/p2ScoreText; drawMultiplayerHUD ranks all live players by score (leader top), renders each in player.color with the local marked '> ... <YOU', charge dots aligned per row (ranked reused). Micro (1 file). EMPIRICAL: tsc -b clean; FULL unit 946/946 (no regression); build 508.29 KB < 550. VISUAL (Pixi = screenshot arbiter): 3-peer e2e re-run PASS 24s + screenshot test-results/s62-3player-hud.png CONFIRMS 3 distinct colored rows RED/CYAN/YELLOW (3rd=yellow) + host <YOU marker + debug panel shows 3 seated players P0/P1/P2. Minor: top-left leaderboard+chargedots+Q-hint share the corner (consistent w/ prior density; spacing=future polish). checkpoint 64e59dc.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #nplayer-seat-assignment-host-authoritative #determinism-ordered-roster-not-map: extended 2->N players by un-hardcoding plumbing (mechanics were already FFA-safe/owner-agnostic - 5 Explore agents found ZERO binary-opponent code). KEY: cross-client determinism comes from the host shipping an ORDERED seat->color roster ARRAY (not a Map - iteration order can diverge) in START_GAME_SIGNAL + every client INSERTING players in seat order. Host freezes peerId<->seat BEFORE broadcasting START so any post-signal INTENT is already stamp-able (no pre-ack buffer needed - ordering resolves Grok's race). Anti-spoof: host stamps INTENT.playerId from sender peerId->seat (intentStamp.ts), so a client can only drive its own seat. Trystero selfId (re-exported via transport) lets each client find its own roster entry. Lesson: generalizing N from 2, the win-condition (max-scan) + per-player Maps usually ALREADY generalize; the real work is seat IDENTITY + the count-hardcoded gates.

P2 #anti-bloat-isNetworked-predicate #radial-spawn-pure-fn-reproduces-binary: replaced ~22 scattered gameMode==='1v1' with isNetworked(world)=gameMode!=='solo' - behavior-identical today but explicit + future-proof, and the per-site AUDIT caught the ONE site that must NOT blanket-swap (connection-lost: peerCount===0 is host-correct but a 3+-player client losing only the host needs explicit host-presence detection - logged P5 gap). radialSpawnPos(seat,total) pure fn REPRODUCES the historical 2-player left/right EXACTLY at N=2 (seat 0 always angle pi=left) so zero regression + all seats equidistant from the contested spawner = FFA-fair. Lesson: a generalized formula that provably reduces to the special case at N=2 beats a branch; Math.round kills cross-client float drift.

P3 #minimal-cosmetic-over-refactor #coverage-by-e2e-not-unit: the N-seat lobby was 90% met by P1 (Trystero room accepts N peers; begin-button latches on first peer) - the only real gap was the one-shot 'Player 2 connected' TEXT. Did the MINIMAL fix (live count text, no constructor refactor) not the full 548-LOC lobbyScreen refactor the Council deferred to P5. The touched method (updatePeerStatus) is untestable in jsdom (needs Pixi) but the multi-peer begin flow it drives is covered by the P4 3-peer e2e at runtime. Lesson: when a 'task' is mostly already-working + the rest is cosmetic, do the cosmetic minimum + let the integration test (not a unit test of an un-unit-testable Pixi method) be the net.

P4 #3-peer-e2e-real-webrtc-arbiter #protocol-bump-breaks-version-e2e: the 3-peer FFA test (3 real Trystero/Nostr contexts) is the definitive proof 1v1v1 works - distinct seats, cross-client color determinism (seat 2=yellow), FFA win. Built trivially on the existing N-page helpers (hostNewRoom/joinRoom/readWorldState/waitForWorld) - the harness was already N-ready. KEY GOTCHA: bumping PROTOCOL_VERSION 3->4 SILENTLY breaks the version-mismatch e2e (they hardcode 'v3' local + a 'v4 newer peer' that's no longer newer); these live in e2e/ which is OUTSIDE tsc -b, so tsc stayed clean while the tests would fail - caught only by RUNNING playwright (the standing 'e2e not in tsconfig -> run Playwright' gotcha). Lesson: any PROTOCOL_VERSION bump MUST sweep e2e/*.spec for hardcoded version literals AND run the version-mismatch specs.

P5 #nplayer-leaderboard-hud-pool #screenshot-as-pixi-arbiter: replaced the hardcoded 2-row RED/BLUE HUD with a pooled MAX_PLAYERS Text leaderboard ranked by score, per-player color, local marked '<YOU' (Gemini quality lift: answers who's-winning + who-am-I in one glance). Pool pattern (create N hidden Text in ctor; show/position/sort the live subset per frame) is the clean way to do dynamic-count Pixi text w/o per-frame alloc. KEY: ui.ts (Pixi) has NO jsdom unit test, so the RUNTIME ARBITER for a HUD change is a SCREENSHOT - added one line to the existing 3-peer e2e (await page.screenshot) + Read the PNG to visually confirm 3 colored rows. Lesson: for un-unit-testable render code, the integration e2e + a screenshot artifact IS the verification; tsc+build only prove it compiles, not that it looks right.

post_ship_analysis: GROK-ANALYST adversarial retrospective (grok-4.20-reasoning t0.3, post-ship). 3 residual risks raised; verified vs source: (R1) cross-version creature desync from ownerPlayerId w/o PROTOCOL_VERSION bump = REFUTED: wire is JSON (parseNetMessage JSON.parse, additive keys ignored by old readers / client ??0); a true mixed S57/S58 match cannot form anyway (S52 already set PROTOCOL_VERSION=3 + detectProtocolMismatch drops stale peer at HELLO); killCount (S36) set the same additive-no-bump precedent. (R2) stuck Carried spark under lost DROP = PARTIALLY REFUTED: existing reconciliation (spark stays in freeSparks + host re-validates next place/grab; joiner applyPerSubstep  guard ends drag on non-Free-non-mine snapshot) covers it, BUT there is NO time-based host TTL auto-release — Council D3 recommended a 5s host backstop and it was deliberately NOT implemented this session (extra tick-loop surface in the highest-risk area; existing reconciliation passed the test matrix). LOGGED CARRY-FORWARD + prime target for the user lossy-network test. (R3) dual simultaneous-grab race = REFUTED by design: host single-threaded, first PICKUP_SPARK wins (state!=Free -> silent reject + raceRejects++), loser self-heals via mine-guard; this IS the double-grab the priority fixes and Sym J e2e exercises it over real WebRTC; raceRejects counter is the instrumentation. NET: 0 ship-stoppers, 1 real carry-forward (host TTL backstop for lossy-release edge).

analyze_summary: S61 ANALYZE (autonomous §XV batch under a non-autonomous-PDR pivot). DEFINING DECISION (correct scope-discipline): the user-approved PDR (fog-feel tuning) REQUIRES the live user-eye (its own arbiter clause), so executing it autonomously = low-quality guesses, against the highest-quality mandate. PIVOTED to the handoff autonomous-SAFE §XV set (S60 precedent), preserved fog as a LOGGED carry-forward (NOT dropped), ran a real Council (Grok+Gemini) as the absent-user adversarial check. WINS: (1) 3 behavior-preserving priorities shipped + CI-green (P1 severBond 19c1245, P2 import-guard e6690b0, P3 worldTypes 19e383e). (2) PRIME-AUDIT empirically REFUTED Grok P1 DEFER via code probe (world.effects is a data array not an emitter -> no re-entrant dispatch; adjacent synchronous reads -> no interleave) while Gemini independently concurred (empirical-beats-reasoning, same as S60). (3) P2 turned BOTH Councils false-confidence critique into a HARDENED transitive guard WITH a positive control proving teeth. (4) P3 used a PROGRAMMATIC byte-exact block move (zero transcription risk) + prod-bundle size as the no-leak arbiter. world.ts 488->286 LOC across P1+P3 (toward 280 charter); dispatch() now uniform; determinism boundary test-locked. (5) Runtime-verified reducer-core surgery via full e2e (15 pass/1 skip) not just tsc, per the handoff standing warning. EFFICIENCY: GREEN throughout (79K->308K, 31%); upfront Council + Explore-agent survey -> no rework/false-starts. METHOD held under autonomy: every priority empirical-green (tsc+unit+e2e+build) before commit; reflexions 3/3. CARRY: fog-feel tuning + lobbyScreen refactor (both need user) + knip inventory.
